heading: Shoreline and riverine protection and restoration
label: 2332 Shoreline and riverine protection and restoration
notes: References in TextSection 160 of the Water Resources Development Act of 2020, referred to in subsec. (g)(1), is , which is set out as a note under .section 160 of div. AA of Pub. L. 116–260section 2201 of this title
CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1999, and not as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 which comprises this chapter.

children.text: reduce flood and hurricane and storm damage hazards; or
restore the natural functions and values of rivers and shorelines throughout the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: the reduction of flood and hurricane and storm damage hazards, including measures for erosion mitigation and bank stabilization; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the conservation and restoration of the natural functions and values of rivers and shorelines.

children.heading: Projects
children.label: B Projects
children.text: Subject to subsection (f)(2), in carrying out subsection (a), the Secretary may design and implement projects described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Consultation and coordination
children.label: 2 Consultation and coordination
children.text: The studies and projects carried out under this section shall be conducted, to the maximum extent practicable, in consultation and coordination with the Federal Emergency Management Agency and other appropriate Federal agencies, and in consultation and coordination with appropriate State and local agencies and tribes.

children.heading: Nonstructural approaches
children.label: 3 Nonstructural approaches
children.text: The studies and projects shall emphasize, to the maximum extent practicable and appropriate, nonstructural approaches to preventing or reducing flood and hurricane and storm damages, including the use of natural features or nature-based features.

children.heading: Participation
children.label: 4 Participation
children.text: The studies and projects shall be conducted, to the maximum extent practicable, in cooperation with State and local agencies and tribes to ensure the coordination of local flood and hurricane and storm damage reduction or riverine, shoreline, and wetland restoration studies with projects that conserve, restore, and manage hydrologic and hydraulic regimes and restore the natural functions and values of floodplains and coastal barriers.

children.heading: Studies
children.label: 1 Studies
children.text: Studies conducted under this section shall be subject to cost sharing in accordance with , except that the first $200,000 of the costs of a study conducted under this section shall be at Federal expense.section 2215 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Design and construction of a project under this section that includes a nonstructural measure, a natural feature or nature-based feature, or an environmental restoration measure, shall be subject to cost sharing in accordance with , except that the non-Federal share of the cost to design and construct such a project benefitting an economically disadvantaged community (including economically disadvantaged communities located in urban and rural areas) shall be 10 percent.section 2213 of this title

children.heading: Items provided by non-Federal interests
children.label: B Items provided by non-Federal interests
children.text: The non-Federal interests shall provide all land, easements, rights-of-way, dredged material disposal areas, and relocations necessary for such projects.

children.heading: Credit
children.label: C Credit
children.text: The value of such land, easements, rights-of-way, dredged material disposal areas, and relocations shall be credited toward the payment required under this paragraph.

children.heading: Structural flood control or hurricane and storm damage reduction projects
children.label: 3 Structural flood control or hurricane and storm damage reduction projects
children.text: Any structural flood control or hurricane and storm damage reduction projects carried out under this section shall be subject to cost sharing in accordance with , except that the non-Federal share of the cost to design and construct such a project benefitting an economically disadvantaged community (including economically disadvantaged communities located in urban and rural areas) shall be 10 percent.section 2213 of this title

children.heading: Operation and maintenance
children.label: 4 Operation and maintenance
children.text: The non-Federal interests shall be responsible for all costs associated with operating, maintaining, replacing, repairing, and rehabilitating all projects carried out under this section.
will significantly reduce potential flood, hurricane and storm, or erosion damages;
will improve the quality of the environment; and
is justified considering all costs and beneficial outputs of the project.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary submits to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a written notification describing the project and the determinations made under subsection (d)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: 21 calendar days have elapsed after the date on which the notification was received by the committees.

children.heading: Projects requiring specific authorization
children.label: 2 Projects requiring specific authorization
children.text: If the Federal share of the cost to design and construct a project under this section exceeds $15,000,000, the Secretary may only carry out the project if Congress enacts a law authorizing the Secretary to carry out the project.

children.heading: Economically disadvantaged community
children.label: 1 Economically disadvantaged community
children.text: The term “economically disadvantaged community” has the meaning given the term as defined by the Secretary under section 160 of the Water Resources Development Act of 2020 ( note).33 U.S.C. 2201

children.heading: Natural feature; nature-based feature
children.label: 2 Natural feature; nature-based feature
children.text: The terms “natural feature” and “nature-based feature” have the meanings given those terms in .section 2289a(a) of this title
